notes: Powerful, structured vintage with exceptional concentration. Profound depth and cellar potential.
Warm, early harvest. Forward and accessible but lacks the density of the great vintages.
Elegant, classical Burgundy character. Good acidity and fine tannin structure.
Difficult growing season but conscientious producers made focused, pure wines with good acidity.
Underrated vintage. Cool growing season produced fresh, precise wines with excellent aging potential.
Rich, concentrated vintage. Dark fruit, excellent structure. One of the finest modern Burgundy vintages.
Hail-ravaged but survivors show beautiful purity. Classic Côte de Nuits character with fine tannins.
Early harvest with ripe, approachable style. More forward than recent vintages but with genuine pleasure.
Voluptuous, opulent vintage. Rich fruit with supple tannins. Broad appeal with serious aging potential.
Extraordinary vintage. Perfect balance of richness and freshness. Potentially the finest of the decade.
Three exceptional vintages in a row. Concentrated and structured with fine acidity preserving freshness.
Challenging frost and mildew year but best producers made classic, cool-climate Pinot Noir of real elegance.
Historic heat waves produced rich, deeply colored wines. Exceptional balance for the vintage character.
Challenging growing season overcame by careful viticulture. Fresh and elegant wines with good typicity.
Powerful, structured vintage across the Côte d'Or. Exceptional depth and longevity.
Warm year. Forward, accessible wines that lack the grip of better vintages.
Elegant and classical. Hail damage reduced yields but quality at the top is high.
Later harvest than usual. Leaner style with high acidity. For early drinking or patient cellaring.
Saved by a magnificent September. Fresh, precise wines with real aging potential.
Rich, voluptuous vintage across the Côte d'Or. Dark fruit, concentration, and excellent structure.
Post-hail survivors show classic, pure Pinot Noir character. Reduced quantities, exceptional quality.
Approachable, fruit-forward style. Early ripening vintage with generous, supple character.
One of the most complete vintages in recent memory. Richness and freshness in harmony.
Potentially the finest Burgundy vintage of the century thus far. Exquisite balance and concentration.
Third consecutive great vintage. Concentrated, structured wines with impressive freshness for the heat.
Difficult spring and summer overcame. Best wines are pure, elegant, and classically Burgundian.
Extreme heat managed well by top producers. Deep, rich wines with impressive structure.
Wet summer but fine autumn saved the vintage. Fresh, aromatic, and classically styled.
Universally considered one of the greatest Pauillac vintages in history. Power, precision, and immortality.
Uneven vintage. Some brilliant wines from top châteaux but inconsistency across the appellation.
Solid, reliable vintage. Good concentration with classic Cabernet structure. Under-appreciated.
Difficult harvest conditions. Lighter style wines for earlier consumption. Best drunk soon.
Underrated vintage of real quality. Classic Left Bank structure with fresh acidity and real longevity.
Magnificent vintage. Rich, concentrated, with exemplary balance. Will age for decades.
Perfect vintage. A once-in-a-generation achievement. Profound concentration and impeccable structure.
Post-frost vintage. Smaller crops but quality is high. Earlier-drinking style than the flanking years.
Rich, powerful, and remarkably complete. One of the greatest modern Bordeaux vintages.
Another outstanding vintage, more classically structured than 2018. Exceptional balance and precision.
Third consecutive exceptional vintage. Concentration meets freshness in a remarkable achievement.
Difficult growing season overcome by meticulous sorting. Fresh, elegant, and surprisingly complete.
Historic heat wave vintage. Remarkably balanced given the conditions. Long-lived and impressive.
Wet conditions overcome with careful canopy management. Fresh, aromatic style with classic structure.
One of the greatest Barolo vintages of all time. Perfect balance of power, tannin, and acidity.
Warm vintage, more opulent and approachable than usual. Forward style but with genuine quality.
Excellent structure and concentration. Classical Barolo character with the substance to age well.
Great traditional vintage. Powerful, tannic, and built for the very long term. A collector's wine.
Wet, difficult vintage. Lighter and earlier-drinking than the great years. Best drunk sooner.
Exceptional warmth produced rich, concentrated Nebbiolo. Superb balance and enormous ageing potential.
Universally acclaimed as a once-in-a-generation vintage. Perfect structure, depth, and longevity.
Hot, drought year. Richer and more concentrated than typical but with good underlying structure.
Return to classical style after heat of 2017. Superb depth, tannin, and acidity. Outstanding.
Elegant, aromatic vintage. Slightly less structured than 2016/2018 but with beautiful finesse.
Warm but not extreme. Approachable style relative to the great vintages with good aging potential.
Cooler vintage with fine freshness and classical aromatic profile. Elegant rather than powerful.
Extreme heat year managed well. Rich, concentrated Nebbiolo with impressive structural integrity.
Near-perfect vintage shared with Barolo. Slightly more accessible earlier given Barbaresco's style.
Warm, forward vintage. More approachable than the great years but with genuine pleasure.
Classical Barbaresco character. Floral, earthy, with firm but refined tannins.
Exceptional structure and depth. One of the finest Barbaresco vintages in decades.
Difficult wet year. Lighter wines more suited to earlier consumption.
Rich, full vintage. Excellent depth and Barbaresco typicity. Fine aging trajectory.
As great as Barolo 2016 — exceptional in every measure. The wine of a lifetime.
Hot year with rich, ripe fruit. More accessible earlier than the classic structure vintages.
Return to classical form. Superb aromatic complexity and structure. Outstanding longevity.
Elegant and finessed. Slightly more forward than 2018 but with genuine class.
Warm but structured. Earlier-drinking than the great years with good typicity.
Cooler, aromatic vintage. Classic floral and earthy character with fine-grained tannins.
Extreme heat managed excellently. Rich, concentrated wines with surprising balance.
Cool growing season produced elegant, structured wines with natural freshness and longevity.
Very cool, late vintage. Leaner style that rewards patience. More Bordeaux-like than typical Napa.
Powerful and concentrated with excellent natural acidity. One of the finest modern Napa vintages.
Back-to-back exceptional vintages. Rich, full-bodied, with superb concentration and structure.
Three consecutive exceptional vintages — a remarkable run. Balanced and ageworthy.
Drought year with concentrated, powerful wines. Slightly lower acidity than the great run but impressive.
Exceptional balance of richness and structure. One of the most age-worthy modern Napa vintages.
Fires late in season affected some estates. Best unaffected wines show exceptional concentration.
Outstanding vintage with perfect ripening conditions. Rich, structured, and beautifully balanced.
Elegant and precise. Cooler year produced more restrained, classically structured wines.
Wildfire smoke impacted many estates. Careful sorting and selection required. Variable quality.
Excellent rebound vintage. Ideal growing conditions produced rich, structured, and age-worthy wines.
Warm but balanced vintage. Powerful, full-bodied wines with impressive structure and longevity.
Widely considered the finest Champagne vintage of the 2000s. Exceptional freshness, tension, and longevity.
Outstanding ripeness and acidity in balance. Profound, complex Champagnes built for the long haul.
Late, cool harvest delivering wines of precision and acidity. Classic, food-friendly character.
Consistent, generous vintage. Good ripeness with the acidity to age well. Reliable quality.
Rich, ripe, and generous. More opulent style than 2012 or 2008 but with impressive complexity.
Small crop but exceptional quality. Concentrated, precise, and beautifully structured. Outstanding longevity.
Good, accessible vintage. More forward character than the great years but with genuine quality.
Generous, ripe vintage matching the quality of 2008 and 2012. Long-lived and impressive.
Warm but fresh vintage with fine balance. Rich, aromatic Champagnes with real aging potential.
Elegant, precise vintage with excellent acidity. Will develop complexity over many years.
The greatest Sauternes vintage since 1990. Perfect botrytis, extraordinary concentration and balance.
Rich, opulent vintage with tremendous concentration. The finest vintage since 2001.
Exceptional balance of botrytis and acidity. Wines of great finesse and longevity.
Good botrytis development produced wines of concentration and complexity. Earlier-drinking style.
Late botrytis harvest yielded wines of real elegance if inconsistent across estates.
Lighter style with less botrytis concentration. Drink sooner rather than later.
Rich, golden vintage with classic Sauternes opulence. Excellent botrytis and aging potential.
Elegant, precise vintage. More restrained than 2015 but with excellent acidity and balance.
Exceptional botrytis conditions in a warm year. Rich, complex wines with impressive longevity.
One of the finest modern Sauternes vintages. Perfect botrytis and exceptional concentration.
Warm vintage with good botrytis. Rich and generous with fine balance.
Warm year producing rich, concentrated Riesling from Spätlese to TBA level. Remarkable botrytis wines.
Perhaps the greatest Mosel vintage in decades. Perfect acidity and Botrytis balance. Wines of extraordinary longevity.
Spring frost impacted yields. Survivors show concentrated, elegant character with fine petrol notes developing.
Drought year. Concentrated, racy Rieslings with steel-like acidity. Will develop magnificently.
Three outstanding vintages in a row. Perfect balance of ripeness and Mosel-typical acidity.
Elegant, precise vintage. Excellent sugar/acid balance producing wines of real finesse and longevity.
Cooler year with pronounced Mosel minerality and acidity. Wines of great typicity and aging potential.
Hot summer produced concentrated, rich Rieslings underpinned by remarkable Mosel acidity. Outstanding.
Unanimously acclaimed as one of the greatest modern Rioja vintages. Power, elegance, and exceptional longevity.
Powerful, concentrated vintage. Full-bodied Tempranillo with deep fruit and firm tannins.
Elegant, precise vintage of real classical Rioja character. Fine tannins and impressive balance.
Three exceptional vintages in close succession. Rich, opulent, and ageworthy in the modern style.
Excellent balance of fruit and structure. Among the finest vintages for Gran Reserva production.
Dry, warm year. Rich and generous but slightly less complexity than the great vintages.
Good Rioja vintage with fresh acidity and fine-grained tannins. Solid aging potential.
Excellent, balanced vintage. Classical Rioja fruit profile with structure supporting long-term development.
Warm but fresh. Concentrated wines with good underlying structure for medium-term cellaring.
Fresher, lighter style. Classical Rioja aromas with good acidity and approachable tannins.
Outstanding, warm vintage for Willamette Pinot Noir. Rich, concentrated, and beautifully structured.
Long, warm growing season. Powerful, concentrated Pinot Noir with depth and excellent aging potential.
Widely considered the greatest modern Willamette vintage. Exceptional balance, structure, and longevity.
Warm, atypically rich vintage for the Valley. More opulent style, best drunk before the decade's end.
Equal to 2014 in many assessments. Perfect growing season, exceptional balance of fruit and acidity.
Smoke taint affected some estates late in harvest. Wines from the best sites remain excellent.
Near-perfect growing season. Rich, concentrated, and elegantly structured. One of the decade's finest.
Long, cool ripening season produced wines of exceptional aromatics, acidity, and structure.
Wildfire smoke a major factor. Many lots declassified. Best unaffected wines retain quality but variable.
Strong rebound vintage. Excellent growing conditions produced structured, aromatic Pinot Noir.
Warm but balanced. Rich, generous Pinot Noir with fine structure and real aging potential.
